parameters.content:
import subprocess, sys, os
try:
 ── Auto-install openpyxl if missing ──────────────────────────────────────────
try:
    import openpyxl
except ImportError:
    subprocess.check_call([sys.executable,'-m','pip','install','openpyxl','--quiet'])
    import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment


def parse_article(raw):
    """
    Splits the article blob that browserReadPage produces into (title, url, summary).

    browserReadPage always stores data in articleStore as a flat string:
        Title: <title>\nURL: <url>\n\n<summary>

    IMPORTANT — the \\n you see in the injected value is intentional.
    On Windows, paths with spaces in the username (e.g. 'C:\\Users\\garv thakre')
    require the entire string to stay on ONE logical line inside the Python source.
    createFile escapes real newlines to \\n so the Python file is syntactically
    valid. openpyxl receives the final string and writes it to the cell correctly.

    This function handles BOTH cases:
      - real newlines  (if running on mac/linux or future fix)
      - literal \\n    (current Windows behaviour — the common case)

    It is intentionally generic so it works for any task: jobs, news,
    apartments, products — anything browserReadPage returns.
    """
    import re
    # Normalise: treat literal \n (two chars) the same as a real newline
    text = raw.replace('\\n', '\n')

    title   = ''
    url     = ''
    summary = ''

    m_title = re.search(r'Title:\s*(.+?)(?:\n|$)', text)
    m_url   = re.search(r'URL:\s*(https?://\S+)', text)
    if m_title:
        title = m_title.group(1).strip()
    if m_url:
        url = m_url.group(1).strip()

    # Everything after the first blank line is the summary
    parts = text.split('\n\n', 1)
    if len(parts) > 1:
        summary = parts[1].strip()
    else:
        # Fallback: strip known prefixes and use what remains
        summary = text
        if title:
            summary = summary.replace(f'Title: {title}', '')
        if url:
            summary = summary.replace(f'URL: {url}', '')
        summary = summary.strip()

    return title, url, summary


# ── Build workbook ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Results'

headers = ['#', 'Title', 'URL', 'Summary']
ws.append(headers)
for cell in ws[1]:
    cell.font      = Font(bold=True, color='FFFFFF')
    cell.fill      = PatternFill('solid', fgColor='1F4E79')
    cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal='center', vertical='center')
ws.row_dimensions[1].height = 22

# ── Data rows — uses split keys populated by browserReadPage automatically ───
# browserReadPage now stores article1_title, article1_url, article1_summary
# in addition to the full article1 blob, so we can use them directly.
# Falls back to parse_article() on the full blob for backward compatibility.
articles = [
    (1, '{{article1_title}}', '{{article1_url}}', '{{article1_summary}}'),
    (2, '{{article2_title}}', '{{article2_url}}', '{{article2_summary}}'),
    (3, '{{article3_title}}', '{{article3_url}}', '{{article3_summary}}'),
]

for num, title, url, summary in articles:
    # If the split keys were not resolved (empty or still a template string),
    # fall back to parsing the full article blob — handles any task generically.
    if not title or title.startswith('{{'):
        raw_blob = locals().get(f'article{num}', '')
        title, url, summary = parse_article(raw_blob)

    ws.append([num, title, url, summary])
    row = ws.max_row
    # Set row height tall enough to show wrapped content
    ws.row_dimensions[row].height = 80
    for col in range(1, 5):
        ws.cell(row=row, column=col).alignment = Alignment(
            wrap_text=True, vertical='top'
        )

# ── Column widths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ws.column_dimensions['A'].width = 4
ws.column_dimensions['B'].width = 40
ws.column_dimensions['C'].width = 45
ws.column_dimensions['D'].width = 80

# ── Save and open ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
path = os.path.expanduser('~/Desktop/Results.xlsx')
wb.save(path)
print('Saved:', path)
if sys.platform == 'win32':
    os.startfile(path)
elif sys.platform == 'darwin':
    subprocess.run(['open', path])

parameters.content:
import subprocess, sys, os, re
try:
    import openpyxl
except ImportError:
    subprocess.check_call([sys.executable,'-m','pip','install','openpyxl','--quiet'])
    import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment


def parse_article(raw):
    """
    Generic parser — works for any browserReadPage output (jobs, news, etc.).
    See browser_search_excel example for full explanation of \\n handling.
    """
    text = raw.replace('\\n', '\n')
    title, url, summary = '', '', ''
    m_title = re.search(r'Title:\s*(.+?)(?:\n|$)', text)
    m_url   = re.search(r'URL:\s*(https?://\S+)', text)
    if m_title: title = m_title.group(1).strip()
    if m_url:   url   = m_url.group(1).strip()
    parts = text.split('\n\n', 1)
    summary = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else text.replace(f'Title: {title}', '').replace(f'URL: {url}', '').strip()
    return title, url, summary


wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Jobs'

headers = ['#', 'Job Title', 'URL', 'Details']
ws.append(headers)
for cell in ws[1]:
    cell.font      = Font(bold=True, color='FFFFFF')
    cell.fill      = PatternFill('solid', fgColor='1F4E79')
    cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal='center', vertical='center')
ws.row_dimensions[1].height = 22

jobs = [
    (1, '{{job1_title}}', '{{job1_url}}', '{{job1_summary}}'),
    (2, '{{job2_title}}', '{{job2_url}}', '{{job2_summary}}'),
    (3, '{{job3_title}}', '{{job3_url}}', '{{job3_summary}}'),
]

for num, title, url, details in jobs:
    if not title or title.startswith('{{'):
        raw_blob = locals().get(f'job{num}', '')
        title, url, details = parse_article(raw_blob)
    ws.append([num, title, url, details])
    row = ws.max_row
    ws.row_dimensions[row].height = 80
    for col in range(1, 5):
        ws.cell(row=row, column=col).alignment = Alignment(wrap_text=True, vertical='top')

ws.column_dimensions['A'].width = 4
ws.column_dimensions['B'].width = 40
ws.column_dimensions['C'].width = 50
ws.column_dimensions['D'].width = 80

path = os.path.expanduser('~/Desktop/LinkedInJobs.xlsx')
wb.save(path)
print('Saved:', path)
if sys.platform == 'win32':
    os.startfile(path)

steps.description: Create a script to generate a job report Excel file
Run the Excel report generator
